United States.summary: Gunman kills multiple people at a California church shooting, authorities say
US Supreme Court declines case over vaccine mandates for federal contractors: report.
US Senate confirms new Secretary of State, Antony Blinken's replacement, Robert Sherman.

South Korea.title: North Korea Fires Multiple Ballistic Missiles
South Korea.summary: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles late at night on July 4, sparking alarm in South Korea and Japan; the missiles flew over the northern region of South Korea before falling into the ocean.

South Korea.title: South Korea to Boost Military Presence in Key Region Amid North Korea Tensions
South Korea.summary: South Korea plans to increase its military presence in a key region near the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) amid rising tensions with North Korea; the decision comes after a series of recent North Korean missile tests.

South Korea.title: South Korea's President to Visit Japan to Promote Peace and Cooperation
South Korea.summary: South Korea's President Yoon Suk-yeol is set to visit Japan this week to promote peace and cooperation between the two nations; the visit comes as relations between the two countries have been strained in recent years.

Japan.title: Japan's PM Fumio Kishida Announces Support for Ukraine in Ongoing Conflict with Russia
Japan.summary: On July 19, 2024, Japan's Prime Minister Fumio Kishida announced his country's support for Ukraine in the ongoing conflict with Russia. This move has increased tensions between Japan and Russia, and the security situation in the region remains unstable.

Japan.title: Tokyo Confirms New COVID-19 Cases Sparking Concerns Amidst Summer Festivals
Japan.summary: On July 22, 2024, Tokyo confirmed new COVID-19 cases, sparking concerns amidst the ongoing summer festivals. The Japanese government has urged residents to remain vigilant and follow health measures to prevent the spread of the disease.

Japan.title: LPG Gas Shortage in Okinawa Due to Increased Demand from Disaster Zones
Japan.summary: On July 20, 2024, a shortage of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) was reported in Okinawa due to increased demand from disaster zones in northern Japan. The shortage has affected households and businesses, and the situation is being closely monitored by the government.
